properties.remark: Multiple reports of ping pong to golfball size hail. Relayed by broadcast media.

Trees down on Needmore Road.
​Barn damage and power line damage blown into roadway near intersection of Levi Beams Road and N Jackson Highway near Magnolia.

An NWS damage survey revealed that a brief EF-1 tornado touched down in NE Hart county. The tornado touched down just southwest of the intersection of US 31E and Levi Beams Road where tree and minor structural damage occurred. The tornado continued eastward snapping and uprooting trees and causing minor barn damage. Peak winds of 95 mph occurred over a 2.7 mile path. The tornado lifted after 3 minutes east of 1079 along Carl Benningfield road.
The tornado lifted 4 SE of Magnolia along Carl Benningfield Road.
Carport pole barn rolled over at residence on Button Knob Road.
Tree damage in roadway between Donansburg and New Salem Church along Donansburg New Salem Road.
3 power poles down along highway 90 east of Burkesville, KY.

The National Weather Service in Louisville confirmed an EF1 tornado in western Simpson County during the late evening hours on February 15th. The tornado was on the ground for just over 4 miles, with peak winds of 90 miles per hour and a maximum width of 150 yards. Please see the associated PNS for additional information.
Partial collapse of a large barn. Time based on radar.

An EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph touched down in West Baden Springs, IN early in the morning on March 15, traveled 11 miles NE and lifted in Orleans, IN approximately 8 minutes later. Along its path, the tornado caused extensive tree damage and occasional structural damage. The most extensive damage was done to a home and several outbuildings near the Lost River area and County Road 100 West.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS survey determined an EF1 tornado with max estimated winds of 100 mph occurred in south Louisville (Jefferson County KY). The length of the tornado was 5.43 miles with a max width of 30 yards. One injury occurred to an individual in Fairdale that was trying to hold a door shut during the peak wind and hurt his arm, but the injury is not serious.

Tornado touched down on south side of Hickory Ridge Road just east of Ten Mile Lane, and then moved NE. Based on damage to trees and barns, intensity was rated EF-1 with max wind speeds near 90 mph. The tornado lifted just south of the intersection of Mill Road and Stewart Ridge Road. Path length 2.5 miles, max width 80 yards.

An NWS storm damage survey determined an EF1 tornado occurred in New Albany. The maximum sustained wind speed was 100 mph and the maximum width was 500 yards, though damage was not continuous over the entire width. Please see the associated public information statement for more details on this tornado.

NWS storm survey team determined an EF3 tornado with 145 mph winds impacted portions of Jefferson County on April 3, 2025. The tornado was 9.68 miles in length and was on the ground for 10 minutes reaching 350 yards in maximum width. Please see the associated PNS for more details about this tornado.

An NWS damage survey concluded that an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 100 mph touched down in northern Harrison county KY near Renaker, and then quickly moved northeast before lifting near Antioch. Along its path, the tornado rolled two separate travel trailers, did some roof damage to a home, damaged trees, and destroyed a small barn. The total path length was over 9 miles with a max width of 50 yards.

*** 1 INJ *** An NWS damage survey concluded that an EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110 mph touched down in far SW Taylor county, and then moved NE over 6 minutes toward and over Green River Lake at times. Along the way numerous trees were snapped, and the most significant damage occurred to the roof of Tuckers Tiki Bar and Restaurant. The tornado lifted at the end of Capps Road. Max path width was 125 yards, and the path length was 6.97 miles long.
Strong winds moved through our neighborhood at this time. With sunrise saw lots of damage in area.

*** 19 FATAL *** Delayed report. This is the starting point of the EF4 tornado which continued through eastern Russell, Pulaski, and Laurel County. The tornado tracked for nearly 60 miles across the three counties, remaining on the ground for nearly 90 minutes. The maximum estimated winds with this tornado were 170 mph, EF4, which occurred in the Sunshine Hills subdivision in London. Please see the associated PNS for additional details.

*** 1 FATAL, 14 INJ *** An NWS damage survey team determined that an EF-2 tornado with peak winds of 125 mph, max width of 450 yards, and track length of 5.55 miles touched down on the morning of Friday May 30th in SE Washington county KY. This tornado completely destroyed two structures along Long Run Road, snapped and uprooted many hardwood and softwood trees, and then did mainly tree damage into Boyle county before it lifted.

An EF 1 tornado touched down briefly west of Nicholasville. It was 20 yards wide with a max wind speed of 90 mph and a length of 0.65 miles.

NWS storm survey team determined an EF1 tornado with maximum winds of 90mph impacted western Nicholasville, KY on October 19, 2025. The tornado began as a cold air funnel, before reaching the ground. The tornado was on the ground for 1 minute and had a maximum width of 20 yards. Please see associated PNS for more details about this tornado. This point marks the starting point of the tornado path.

An NWS damage survey determined that a brief EF-0 tornado touched down about 1.5 miles ENE of Buffalo. Peak winds were 85 mph with a path width of 20 yards and a two mile long path. Most of the impacts from the tornado consisted of a mixture of trees down, barns collapsed, and shingle damage to some homes.

Brief EF0 tornado with peak winds around 75 mph touched down near the intersection of Lickford Bridge and Hill Grove Rds northwest of Central, IN. Most noticeable damage was a livestock barn that got lifted downstream into a cluster of small trees as well as a storage shed that got thrown left of the track about a 100 yards. Homeowners were in the building when it happened but home had no visible damage. Tornado was on the ground for about a tenth of a mile with a maximum width of 60 yards. Time of starting point estimated from radar.

Retransmit of previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 N Elk Horn. Fencing blown over as well as a few trees downed and some shingle damage on a roof at another home on Franklin Rd. Nearby KAAS reported a lengthy period of higher winds. 1055 PM observation had SW winds at 48 mph and 1115 PM had NW winds at 47 mph. Time of this damage estimated from radar.
